mission.title: thanos: [Feature] Per-Store TLS configuration in Thanos Query
mission.description: [Feature] Per-Store TLS configuration in Thanos Query. Requested by 92+ users.

steps.title: Check current thanos deployment
steps.description:
Verify your thanos version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n thanos -l app.kubernetes.io/name=thanos
helm list -n thanos 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working thanos installation.

steps.title: Review thanos configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant thanos configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n thanos -l app.kubernetes.io/name=thanos
kubectl get configmap -n thanos -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=thanos
```
## Request
Feature request to add support for per-store tls configuration.

## Use Case
I have `store` running alongside `query` on my main "monitoring" cluster and we don't use TLS for comms between pods on the same cluster.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Feature] Per-Store TLS configuration in Thanos Query
Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n thanos -l app.kubernetes.io/name=thanos
kubectl get events -n thanos --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[Feature] Per-Store TLS configuration in Thanos Query" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Why does it need that?

I think you should be able to proxy all the traffic to GPRC backend.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with thanos installed or the issue environment reproducible.
